source_ref: [GAP — Mexico's own research is dog-specific only]

fact_label: Pet import (dog) — rabies titer / quarantine requirement
value_raw: No rabies titer required; no quarantine if compliant
notes: Current rabies vaccination, parasite treatment within 6 months, tick treatment shortly before entry, ISO microchip (recommended) — a materially easier process than many destinations in this project

fact_label: Private health insurance — new-enrollment age ceiling
notes: Existing policyholders typically keep renewing past the ceiling; someone not already enrolled loses access to most standard plans past it; premiums rise sharply from the early 60s regardless; international (non-domestic) insurance is the named workaround. Archetype/age-neutral fact — a real compound gate for some readers, irrelevant to others
Range driven by age; national, applies uniformly to all five scored locations
Remarkably low by US/European standards — a real, durable structural advantage

value_raw: No nationwide mandate from the immigration authority (INM); consulate-dependent, increasing for Europe/Canada/some US jurisdictions
notes: Checked directly this pass rather than assumed — a real but not uniform compound-gate risk on top of the age-enrollment-ceiling row above; the immigration authority (INM) can also request updated proof at renewal
Fideicomiso bank-trust workaround inside the zone; ~$500-3,000 setup, ~$500-1,000/yr ongoing, ~21,650 MXN foreign-affairs ministry (SRE) permit fee
Unlike Guatemala's territorial system — worldwide income taxed once resident

fact_label: Temporary Resident Visa accepts passive income as qualifying proof
notes: Source file's own open question: whether passive/non-employment income qualifies as documentable "income" under this route vs. requiring the savings-based alternative

fact_label: Temporary Resident Visa converts to permanent residency
notes: 4 years continuous temporary residency converts without re-proving solvency — a real on-ramp, not Colombia's V/M dead-end shape
Roughly triple Guatemala's Rentista threshold
Income or savings, not combined — this route's own structure requires one pool to clear the bar, not two separate ones
Plus ~500-1000/yr ongoing, and ~21,650 MXN foreign-affairs ministry (SRE) permit fee, already in the original row's own notes.
Communal tenure, not lawfully transferable title to a foreign buyer without formal conversion (dominio pleno).
